title: cyathea tahitensis
intro: Cyathea tahitensis, also known as Tahitian fern or Tahiti tree fern, is a species of fern native to the Pacific Islands, specifically Tahiti and other islands in French Polynesia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The extracts of Cyathea tahitensis have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and the leaves are also used to make a poultice or topical treatment for skin conditions.
